name: Dr Michael Shafer
focus: Robotics and mechatronics • UAV telemetry • Energy harvesting
bio: Michael W. Shafer is the Associate Dean for Research in Northern Arizona University's Steve Sanghi College of Engineering, a Professor of Mechanical Engineering, and Associate Director for Engineering at the Radiant Center for Remote Sensing. He earned his Ph.D. in Mechanical Engineering from Cornell University and began his career at NASA's Jet Propulsion Laboratory, where he contributed to the Mars Science Laboratory mission. Most recently, he served as principal investigator on a major semiconductor workforce and research laboratory development grant, leading initiatives in new degree programs, apprenticeships, research facilities, and strategic labor planning to address critical workforce needs in advanced manufacturing. Shafer's research leadership spans wildlife telemetry systems, UAV development, and energy harvesting technologies, including an open-source UAV radio telemetry system that has been deployed by wildlife researchers internationally. As Associate Dean for Research, he oversees research enterprise growth, faculty mentorship programs, and strategic proposal development across the college. He has managed more than $16 million in external funding from the National Science Foundation, the Arizona Commerce Authority, and industry partners, while advancing remote sensing and drone technologies with applications in environmental monitoring, infrastructure assessment, and resource management.
